From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

British trade union leader (1924–1989)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **relies largely or entirely on a [single source](/wiki/Wikipedia:Articles_with_a_single_source "Wikipedia:Articles with a single source")**. Relevant discussion may be found on the [talk page](/wiki/Talk:David_Basnett "Talk:David Basnett"). Please help [improve this article](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=David_Basnett&action=edit) by [introducing citations to additional sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). *Find sources:* ["David Basnett"](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22David+Basnett%22) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22David+Basnett%22+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22David+Basnett%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22David+Basnett%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22David+Basnett%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22David+Basnett%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(July 2014)* |

**David Basnett, Baron Basnett** (9 February 1924 – 25 January 1989) was a British trade union leader.

Born in [Liverpool](/wiki/Liverpool "Liverpool"), Basnett studied at [Quarry Bank High School](/wiki/Quarry_Bank_High_School "Quarry Bank High School") before becoming a bank clerk. He served as a pilot with the [Royal Air Force](/wiki/Royal_Air_Force "Royal Air Force") during [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II"), then in 1948 began working for the [National Union of General and Municipal Workers](/wiki/National_Union_of_General_and_Municipal_Workers "National Union of General and Municipal Workers") (NUGMW), as their regional officer for Liverpool.[[1]](#cite_note-odnb-1)

In 1955, Basnett became the NUGMW's national education officer, while, in 1960, he was appointed national industrial officer, and in 1966 he was elected to the general council of the [Trades Union Congress](/wiki/Trades_Union_Congress "Trades Union Congress") (TUC). In 1970, he negotiated a satisfactory settlement to a major dispute at [Pilkington](/wiki/Pilkington "Pilkington"), and he used this success as a springboard to win election as the union's general secretary in 1973.[[1]](#cite_note-odnb-1)

As general secretary, Basnett pursued a campaign of centralisation, aimed to increase participation, and conducted numerous mergers with smaller unions, the NUGMW changing its name twice under his leadership, becoming the "General, Municipal, Boilermakers and Allied Trades Union" in 1982. He served as [President of the TUC](/wiki/President_of_the_TUC "President of the TUC") in 1978, and was also on the [National Enterprise Board](/wiki/National_Enterprise_Board "National Enterprise Board") and the [National Economic Development Council](/wiki/National_Economic_Development_Council "National Economic Development Council").[[1]](#cite_note-odnb-1)

In 1979, Basnett founded **Trade Unionists for a Labour Victory**, and spent much of the next decade promoting conference between trade union and [Labour Party](/wiki/Labour_Party_(UK) "Labour Party (UK)") members. He took early retirement in 1986, frustrated at the lack of progress from these initiatives. He was created a [life peer](/wiki/Life_peer "Life peer") on 31 March 1987 taking the title **Baron Basnett**, *of [Leatherhead](/wiki/Leatherhead "Leatherhead"), in the [County of Surrey](/wiki/County_of_Surrey "County of Surrey")*.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

Lord Basnett died on 25 January 1989, at the age of 64.[[1]](#cite_note-odnb-1)
